pages.raw_text:
The referendum ordinance that
has slumbered peacefully in the
committee Of the whole for sever
al months finally came to the
front last night. The ordinance
provides that whenever 10 per
cent of the voters 80 desire they
can secure a referendum vote on
all public utility franchises. The
ordinance was introduced by
Councilman Pratt and went to
committee of the whole. Last
night the ordinance was discussed
favorably and sent to Corporation
Counsel Geraghty to be put in
conformity with city ordinances.
The ordinance will be made a
special order at some future meet
ing of the council, and arguments
win be heard by advocates and
those who may be opposed.


Several of the councilmen have
expressed themselves favorable to
the referendum on the ground that
1t will relieve them Of great
measure of personal responsibility
by indicating just what action the
people desire taken on important
measures.
